prose:
Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans‐style orbital energy difference) = 8.9406687 eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied gas-phase water geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz); no re-optimization performed (notation: DFT//input xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell RHF/RKS)
Software: PySCF (via chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill)
Convergence & diagnostics: SCF converged in 7 cycles; integrity.status = ok; integrity.trustworthy = true; no warnings reported.

Key orbital energies (eV)
• HOMO (index 4): −8.6037183 eV
• LUMO (index 5):  +0.33695037 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap: 8.9406687 eV

Reproducibility
• Command (from cli_invocation):
  chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz --out <result>.json
• Result JSON written to: /workspace/water_dft_frontier.json
• A live PySCF stdout/stderr log was streamed to a frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory during execution and can be inspected for SCF details.
